dominants.dominant_desc:
In the early game, focus on safe farming in the Gold Lane. Avoid unnecessary fights and always maintain distance from aggressive heroes. Use Skill 1 to poke enemies and secure minions safely. Positioning is very important because Lesley is vulnerable in the early stage.

During mid game, prioritize farming and joining small team fights only when it is safe. Stay behind your tank and deal damage from a safe distance. Use your Ultimate to finish low-HP enemies or force them to retreat.

In the late game, positioning becomes the key to victory. Always stay at the backline and wait for enemies to use their crowd control skills before entering fights. With proper positioning and timing, Lesley can eliminate enemy carries quickly and turn the fight in your team's favor.
Survival and positioning are the most important factors to maximize Lesley's damage potential.

emblems.emblem_desc:
I chose the Marksman Emblem to maximize Lesley's damage output and scaling potential.

The extra Attack Speed helps her trigger passive damage more frequently, while the Adaptive Attack increases her overall burst potential. The Lifesteal also improves her sustainability in lane and during extended fights.

This emblem setup enhances her late-game power spike, allowing her to deal consistent critical damage while staying safer in team fights. It perfectly supports Lesley's role as a high-damage late-game carry.

equips.equip_desc: Other

hero.hero_overview:
Lesley is a high burst Marksman who specializes in critical damage and long-range sniping. She is best played in the Gold Lane and becomes extremely powerful in the late game. With proper farming, Lesley can eliminate squishy enemies in just a few shots.

She excels at poking safely from a distance and finishing off low-HP targets.
hero.hero_strength:
Extremely high Critical Damage in late game

Long attack range, making her safe in team fights

Skill 1 increases damage and provides camouflage for safe poking

Ultimate can lock onto enemies and finish low-health targets

Can deal massive damage even to tanks in late game

If farmed well, Lesley becomes one of the most dangerous late-game carries.
hero.hero_weakness:
Very squishy and vulnerable to assassins

Weak in the early game

No strong dash skill for escape

Easily countered by heavy crowd control

(CC)

spell.spell_desc:
I chose Flicker to improve Lesley's survivability and positioning. Since Lesley has no strong dash skill, Flicker helps her escape from assassins and dangerous crowd control situations. It also allows her to reposition quickly during team fights to maintain a safe distance while dealing damage.

Flicker can be used aggressively to secure kills by closing the gap and finishing enemies with enhanced basic attacks or Ultimate. However, it is mainly used as a defensive spell to avoid sudden attacks and survive longer in late game fights.

With proper timing, Flicker makes Lesley much safer and more effective as a late-game carry.

equips.equip_desc: Movement & damage

Lesley is a marksman hero known for her high damage output and ability to take down enemies from a distance

Lesley's basic attacks grant energy. If she doesn't take damage for 5 seconds, her next basic attack has increased range, a 40% critical chance, and deals 1.3x damage. Each fixed physical penetration also gives Lesley a 1% additional critical rate
equips.equip_title: Lesley One Hit Build In Late game

hero.hero_overview: Lesley is a marksman hero known for her high damage output and ability to take down enemies from a distance
hero.hero_strength: Lesley's basic attacks grant energy. If she doesn't take damage for 5 seconds, her next basic attack has increased range, a 40% critical chance, and deals 1.3x damage. Each fixed physical penetration also gives Lesley a 1% additional critical rate
hero.hero_weakness:
- No CC (crowd control) skills.
- Vulnerable to ambushes, bursts, and CC effects
- Item dependent.
- Ultimate can be interrupted by stun, knockback, suppression, airborne, transform, or freeze effects

emblems.emblem_desc: These emblems help Lesley so much during the game because she gained extra crit chance and extra crit damage by Fotal and this is useful for finish an enemy with extra burst damage, she can get her equipments faster with Bargain Hunter so this help her when she fights, Lesley can be also restored some hp with Quantum charge and its increased when she gets the rapid boots with extra movement speed when hitting enemy with basic attack.

equips.equip_desc: Berserker's fury for extra crit damage and chance , Sky piercer for finish the enemy and get 10 stacks by each killfir each death, Rapid boots for extra movement speed when leave the fight and restore a little amount of hp, Blade of despair for extra physical attack , Malefic gun for extra basic attack range, and Blade of the heptaseas for extra physical attack and Extra max hp.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Shields + heals + CC make Lesley survive longer and deal safe damage.
Great frontliner; sets up fights so Lesley can free-hit.

counters.counter_desc: Stealth burst easily kills Lesley if unprotected.
High mobility and burst damage punish squishy marksmen.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Focus on last-hitting minions to speed up item farming.

Avoid direct fights since Lesley is weak early on.

Use her long attack range to poke and pressure enemies safely.
With 2,3 core items, Lesley gains strong power spikes. Focus on joining ganks, using your Ultimate to secure kills, and staying in the backline while providing vision with her passive.

Lesley shines as a top damage dealer in late game.

Prioritize positioning: stay behind your tanks and avoid moving alone.

Use the first shot after camouflage (Passive) for massive critical damage.

Main priority: target enemy squishy heroes (marksmen or mages).

emblems.emblem_desc: This setup makes Lesley faster, stronger, and harder to catch. It amplifies her natural strengths (crit damage + long range) while giving her some crowd-control utility to help in fights.

equips.equip_desc:
Lesley is a crit-based marksman who relies on basic attacks to deal huge bursts of damage. She is weak early but becomes a late-game sniper if built correctly.

👉 This build was chosen to:

Boost her critical damage (her main strength).

Give sustain and lifesteal for farming safely.

Provide armor penetration to fight tanks.

Maximize her late-game one-shot potential with the highest attack item.

hero.hero_overview: Lesley is a Marksman specialized in long-range critical hits and sniping. She usually plays in the Gold Lane, focusing on farming early to scale into a strong late-game damage dealer.
hero.hero_strength:
Long attack range, safe poking.

High damage in mid/late game.

Ultimate can finish fleeing enemies.

Has stealth skill for repositioning.
hero.hero_weakness:
Weak and vulnerable in early game.

Limited mobility compared to other Marksmen.

Easily targeted by Assassins and burst damage.

Highly dependent on positioning and farming.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker lets Lesley quickly teleport a short distance, perfect for escaping or chasing enemies.

hero.hero_overview: Lesley is a deadly marksman hero in Mobile Legends, known for her high attack damage and long-range precision.
hero.hero_strength:
Strengths

Long-range attacks- Lesley has the longest attack range in the game, making her a great pick for poking enemies from a safe distance.
High damage output- Her passive skill, Lethal Shot, deals true damage and penetrates physical damage, making her a threat to even the tankiest heroes.
Mobility- Lesley's Master of Camouflage skill allows her to become invisible and move quickly around the map, making her hard to catch.
Burst damage- Her Ultimate Snipe skill deals massive damage to enemies, especially those with low health.
hero.hero_weakness:
Weaknesses

Vulnerability to burst damage- Lesley is weak against heroes who can burst her down quickly, such as assassins and fighters with high damage output.
Lack of crowd control- Lesley doesn't have any crowd control skills, making her vulnerable to Heroes
 who can lock her down.
Item dependency- Lesley's damage output relies heavily on her items, making her less effective without the right build.
Durability- Lesley has low durability and can be easily taken out if she's not careful.

data.recommend: I hope it will help you, in early game don't fight
data.title: Lesley build

cooperates.cooperate_desc: They protect Lesley and create space in teamfights, letting her snipe safely from the back.
Lesley thrives on map awareness—vision lets her position safely and catch low-health targets.
Their lockdown makes it easier for Lesley to land her ultimate or clean up kills from afar.

counters.counter_desc: Lesley can outrange and poke them safely
Easy to burst down with passive crits or ultimate.
They can burst her before she can escape.
Can expose her even when camouflaged or snipe her back.
Hard to kill without anti-heal or team coordination.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Stay Behind Minions: Lesley is squishy early. Always position behind your wave to avoid early poke or engage from enemy laners.

Play Around Passive: Lesley's passive grants enhanced range, crit, and damage after being out of combat—poke with this whenever safe, then back off.

Watch the Mini-map Constantly: Early jungle ganks can easily kill Lesley. If the enemy jungler is missing, play under tower or hug the wall for a quick escape path

Hero-Specific Early Tips & Tricks:
Poke them using passive shots after camo. You can zone them slightly without hard committing.

Always keep Skill 2 (Grenade) ready to disengage. Save Flicker if CC is imminent.

Focus on farming clearing lane and wait for support help or level 4 power spike.

Teamfight Tips (Pre-Level 4 Skirmishes):
Use passive-enhanced shots to chip down enemies before full fights begin. Avoid diving into fights early.

Camo can be used to scout nearby fights or objectives early—just be cautious of AoE skills or vision traps.

Let your roamers or EXP laner take the first hit,
dominants.dominant_title: Lesley is weak early, so avoid risky trades. Use her long basic attack range and passive crits to last hit minions and lightly poke the enemy

emblems.emblem_desc: Because Lesley relies on burst damage and item scaling to eliminate enemies quickly from a distance.

equips.equip_desc:
Magic Shoes: +10% cooldown reduction helps spam skills, especially her camouflage and grenade for mobility and repositioning.

Sky Piercer: Boosts crit chance and movement speed, enabling faster positioning and stronger basic attacks—perfect for her passive.

Berserker Fury: Core item for Lesley. Greatly boosts crit damage and synergizes with her passive to deal massive burst damage.

Sea Halberd: Provides anti-heal, useful against lifesteal-heavy heroes, while still giving decent physical attack and attack speed.

Endless Battle: Adds true damage, lifesteal, and sustain. It also provides extra damage after skills—great after camouflage or grenade usage.

Blade of Despair (BOD): Huge damage spike. It amplifies damage on low-HP targets, which works perfectly with her ult and passive to finish kills.


This build gives Lesley high burst, sustain, cooldown, and utility ideal for staying mobile and lethal

hero.hero_overview: Lesley, known as the "Deadly Sniper," is a Marksman/Assassin hybrid in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang.  She excels at long-range engagements and delivering high burst damage, making her a formidable presence in the Gold Lane.
hero.hero_strength:
Long-Range Poke: Her passive gives her extended attack range and bonus damage, allowing her to poke enemies safely.

High Burst Damage: With critical builds, she can quickly eliminate squishy targets, especially when using her passive and ultimate effectively.

Camouflage and Mobility: Her Skill 1, Master of Camouflage, allows her to become invisible and move faster, making her hard to catch or target in fights.

Safe Finisher: Her Ultimate, Ultimate Snipe, lets her finish off fleeing enemies from long range without putting herself in danger.
hero.hero_weakness:
Weak Early Game: Low damage and reliance on basic attacks make her vulnerable early on.

Squishy and Easy to Kill: She has no hard escape skills and is easily burst down if caught.

Reliant on Positioning: Poor positioning can render her useless or lead to quick deaths.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker can be combined with Tactical Grenade to create unexpected movement, confusing enemies or extending her escape path.

data.title: Silent Shot: The Ultimate Lesley Guide

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Tigreal can initiate team fights with his ultimate, allowing Lesley to deal massive damage from a safe distance. His skill set also provides crowd control, which helps protect Lesley.

counters.counter_desc: Lesley can deal significant damage from a safe distance, making it difficult for Gatotkaca to get close.

dominants.dominant_desc:
- Positioning: Stay behind your frontlines and use your long attack range to deal damage safely. Avoid getting close to enemies, especially assassins.
 
- Skill Sequence: Start with Lethal Shot (Skill 1) to poke enemies, then use Tactical Grenade (Skill 2) to slow them down and enhance your damage. Use Ultimate Snipe (Skill 3) when enemies engage or disengage.
 
- Item Build: Prioritize damage and attack speed items to maximize your damage output. A recommended build includes Swift Boots, Berserker's Fury, Scarlet Phantom, and Blade of Despair. Adjust your build based on the enemy composition.
 
- Map Awareness: Keep an eye on the minimap and use Lesley's passive to scout bushes. Be aware of enemy movements to avoid ganks.
 
- Use Flicker or Sprint: These spells can help you escape dangerous situations or chase down enemies.

emblems.emblem_desc: This emblem maximize Lesley's damage.

equips.equip_desc: This help Lesley to maximize her damage

hero.hero_overview: Lesley is one of the few marksmen in the game who use Energy instead of Mana. Her passive, Lethal Shot, allows her to deal 100% critical damage after gaining extra energy, making her a sniper-type hero rather than a typical ADC (Attack Damage Carry).
hero.hero_strength: Lesley's strength in Mobile Legends lies in her ability to deal significant true damage and critical damage, making her a powerful single-target threat. Her ultimate, Ultimate Snipe, allows her to lock onto an enemy and fire four fatal bullets in succession, dealing substantial damage, especially against tanks. Additionally, her passive, Lethal Shot, grants her 100% critical damage after accumulating energy, enhancing her sniper-like abilities.
hero.hero_weakness: Lesley's main weaknesses in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang include her vulnerability to being focused and her reliance on kiting to stay alive. Her lack of strong early game presence and reliance on her ultimate for significant damage also make her susceptible to counters.

spell.spell_desc: Lesley in Mobile Legends benefits from the Flicker battle spell due to her lack of mobility and her ability to engage in a burst damage playstyle. Flicker provides a swift repositioning option, allowing her to engage fights, dodge incoming damage, or quickly escape dangerous situations. This flexibility is crucial for a marksman who relies on maintaining distance and dealing high damage from afar.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Grock clears the minion wave very fast, best combination for Lesley to get item early game!

counters.counter_desc: They can all in on Lesley , she will die and Angela will die too.

dominants.dominant_desc: Buy boots, do not upgrade the boots, buy windtalker and farm, play safe under tower, steal gold crab using charged passive with your enhance basic attack, first skill and last hit the crab when low.

If you have the berserker fury in mid game, you can now damage the enemy with basic attack + first skill basic attack to cancel the basic attack animation; to double attack faster. But before engaging, scan the area using your ult first before you fight, always watch for assassins, they are your greatest threat and the initiator roamer. Stay away from your mage teammate and other squishy teammate to avoid being killed all at once. But always be around them.

Use ult to scan enemies but do not shoot them, it will increase the cd of your ultimate, use it when your doing lord to give advance visions to your team, if they start moving to your location, cancel your ultimate and use first skill to reposition yourself and be ready for fight. Do not engage, stay hidden, attack only when they did not notice you. When they start chasing you, reposition using your first skill to move away from them or flicker if needed.

emblems.emblem_desc: Why common emblem? Lesley clears minions very slow unlike other marksman s. Which means she will get bullied in early game so she will not get most of farm in gold lane. In order to fix this problem, common emblem hp regen and quantum charge helps her to stay alive and not go back in the base, the mystery shop helps Lesley to get the item as fast as possible. So in mid game and late game she will not fall behind.

equips.equip_desc: Why windtalker first? Like I said, Lesley early game farming is very difficult, windtalker will help her farm faster than buying berserker fury first or endless battle. This is the standard Lesley build, 20% cooldown reduction means you will have balance cd for your first skill (your main damage dealing enhancement ability) maléfic gun for extra range attack and bod for extra damage.
Wind of nature for 2 seconds physical damage immunity, you can swap this to rose gold meteor if the assassin is magic damage dealer.

hero.hero_overview: Lesley is a unique marksman that mainly uses first skill to deal damage to enemies or to use it as an escape tool. She can knock back the enemy in front of her and her ultimate can be use to scan the enemy around her or to use it to finish the enemy from a far.
hero.hero_strength: True damage is very hard to counter in the game, there are very limited items that counters true damage. Another strength of Lesley is that she can turn comouflage to avoid direct spells or abilities. One of the example is Zilong first skill or 2nd skill, Lesley cannot be targeted while she is in comouflage mode (first skill)
hero.hero_weakness: Lesley weakness is that she is very item dependent, which means that she is very weak at early game, if she fails to buy important items in early game, she will fall behind in mid game and late game. Another thing is that Lesley does not benefit a lot from attack speed, she is a skill type hero like Brody, but the big difference between them is that she does not deal more damage than Brody because they both have different passive ability.

data.recommend: I highly recommend this guide, clear, concise and very helpful!

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is the best spell for Lesley since she does not benefit alot from inspire unlike most of the marksman can benefit from it. She can use flicker and first skill to stay hidden and avoid direct target abilities and spells. She can also use it to get close to enemy to finish them off but only use it if you think your safe.
